channel.title: Nature Communications
channel.description:
Nature Communications is an open access, multidisciplinary journal dedicated to publishing high-quality research in all areas of the biological, health, physical, chemical and Earth sciences. Papers published by the journal aim to represent important advances of significance to specialists within each field.

We are committed to providing an efficient service for both authors and readers. Our team of independent editors make rapid and fair publication decisions. Prompt dissemination of accepted papers to a wide readership and beyond is achieved through a programme of continuous online publication.
channel.dc:rights: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.
channel.prism:copyright: © 2025 Macmillan Publishers Limited, part of Springer Nature. All rights reserved.

item.title: Momentum space AC Josephson effect and intervalley coherence in multilayer graphene
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67838-7

The authors propose that when an intervalley coherent (IVC) state is subjected to a static magnetic field, it develops an oscillating orbital magnetization at microwave frequency, an effect analogous to a Josephson oscillation in momentum space. The effect could provide an experimental diagnostic for identifying the IVC state.
item.dc:title: Momentum space AC Josephson effect and intervalley coherence in multilayer graphene
item.dc:creator: Mainak Das
Chunli Huang

item.title: Demonstrating quantum error mitigation on logical qubits
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67768-4

Quantum error mitigation refers to techniques that reduce, rather than correct, errors in quantum computing. Here the authors demonstrate zero-noise extrapolation applied to quantum error correction circuits on superconducting processors, effectively reducing logical errors and advancing early fault-tolerant quantum computing.
item.dc:title: Demonstrating quantum error mitigation on logical qubits
item.dc:creator: Aosai Zhang
Haipeng Xie
Yu Gao

item.title: Cavity-QED-controlled two-dimensional Moiré excitons without twisting
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67570-2

The authors show that structured optically driven cavities can recreate Moiré-like exciton localization and that cavity vacuum fluctuations induce long-range exciton interactions, modifying exciton masses and optical properties without twisting.
item.dc:title: Cavity-QED-controlled two-dimensional Moiré excitons without twisting
item.dc:creator: Francesco Troisi
Hannes Hübener
Angel Rubio
Simone Latini

item.title: Winding coupling phase for pseudo-spin-derived topological photonics
item.content:encoded:
Nature Communications, Published online: 24 December 2025; doi:10.1038/s41467-025-67627-2

Topological phases derived from spin and pseudo-spin dynamics are generally described by case-specific models. Here, authors propose a general theoretical framework that enables a flexible design of a variety of pseudo-spin-induced topological phases including spin-valley Hall phase, anomalous Hall phase and anti-helical edge states.
item.dc:title: Winding coupling phase for pseudo-spin-derived topological photonics
item.dc:creator: Tianyuan Liu
Min Qiu
Wei Yan
